attacking_style: Pure sprinter who excels in reduced bunch sprints after selective climbs eliminate pure speed specialists. Lacks explosive final 200m acceleration to win from perfect positioning, relying instead on tactical superiority and endurance to outlast faster rivals.
team_tactics: Heavily dependent on full leadout trains that frequently deliver perfect positioning but fail conversion. Teams commit all resources (Trek-Segafredo, Israel Premier Tech) to control finals, but leadout failures or isolation in headwinds repeatedly cost results.
terrain_preference: Selective stages with tactical climbing that shed pure sprinters but don't require sustained high tempo. Thrives in reduced group sprints from breakaway catches or after small time gaps form. Technical finales expose acceleration deficiency.
vulnerability: Sustained high-tempo pace-making before sprints (dropped when BORA set tempo despite support). Insufficient final 200m acceleration when leadout fails—repeatedly holds perfect position but cannot come around rivals. Accumulates near-misses under pressure.
key_patterns: Chronic second-place pattern: 11th Giro runner-up finish, 16-podium winless drought before 2021 breakthrough
Perfect positioning, insufficient acceleration: held dead-center/optimal wheels but lacked final kick in multiple races
Leadout dependency critical: isolation or technical zone failures directly correlate with poor results across teams
Vulnerable when sustained tempo precedes sprint: dropped by BORA pace-making, needs tactical not tempo racing
Mental resilience: rebounded from disappointments to win chaotic sprints, broke winless drought under pressure
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 showed podium capability after selective climbing but Israel Premier Tech leadout failures continued. 2021 DNS suggests fragility, though Verona win proved top-end improvement. Pattern of strong positioning without conversion persists.
